Define the custom token classes for passthrough entities: XImage, XPerson, XCommentRef, XSoftBreak, XPageBreak, XChip. These are mistletoe SpanTokens that match \u003cx-*\u003e HTML-like tags.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T06:24:23Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:24:23Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.1","title":"Bug: retrieve_auth_code always returns None — Phase 1 CLI flow broken","description":"retrieve_auth_code() in database.py validates 'service_account_email' is non-empty, but the field is intentionally stored as empty string since SA provisioning was deferred to on_session_establishment().

This means every POST /api/auth/session/exchange call returns HTTP 400 'Invalid or expired auth code' — the Phase 1 CLI flow is completely broken in this branch.","notes":"Root cause: database.py:245 checks 'if not service_account_email or not expires_at:' but service_account_email is always '' per the design decision in this branch.

Fix: Remove the 'not service_account_email' guard from retrieve_auth_code(). The SA email field is intentionally blank — SA provisioning happens later in on_session_establishment(). The only required fields are user_email and expires_at.

Affected code:
- database.py:245 — remove service_account_email from the guard check
- Also verify save_auth_code still stores user_email correctly (it does)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:07Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:07Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.2","title":"Bug: refresh token rotation overwrites full consented scopes with command-minimum scopes","description":"When Google issues a rotated refresh token, generate_oauth_token() in token_generator.py stores only the minimum scopes for the current command — not the full originally-consented scopes.

After rotation, the stored refresh_token_scopes in Firestore reflects only the last-called command's scopes (e.g., 'calendar'). Subsequent consent checks in credential_router.resolve() will 403 for all other scopes (gmail, spreadsheets, etc.), forcing users to re-login even though they already consented.","notes":"Root cause: token_generator.py:481:
  await self._db.set_refresh_token(user_email, new_encrypted, ' '.join(scopes))
where 'scopes' is the minimum scope list for that specific command.

Fix options:
1. Read the existing scopes from Firestore before writing, then union with the current scopes.
2. Never overwrite scopes during rotation — only update the encrypted_token field. The scopes were set at consent time and should not change.

Option 2 is simpler and more correct. Add a separate set_refresh_token_only() method (or update set_refresh_token to have scopes=None meaning 'do not update scopes'), OR call set_service_account_email-style merge that preserves the scopes field.

Simplest fix: In generate_oauth_token(), when storing the rotated token, read the existing record's scopes first and pass those instead of the command scopes:
  record = await self._db.get_refresh_token(user_email)
  await self._db.set_refresh_token(user_email, new_encrypted, ' '.join(record.scopes))

This preserves the original consented scopes across rotations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:20Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:20Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.3","title":"Bug: scope allowlist uses delegation_scopes for OAuth commands — wrong config key","description":"credential_router.resolve() enforces the scope allowlist by calling settings.is_scope_allowed(), which checks against DELEGATION_SCOPES. But in oauth mode, SA commands have scopes like 'spreadsheets' and 'documents' that are not in DELEGATION_SCOPES.

This works silently today only because DELEGATION_SCOPES defaults to empty (allow all). If an operator sets DELEGATION_SCOPES to lock down DWD commands, they will unexpectedly also block OAuth SA commands for sheets, docs, etc.","notes":"Root cause: credential_router.py:183 — is_scope_allowed() reads delegation_scopes config.

The scope allowlist in OAuth mode should be the OAUTH_SCOPES setting, not DELEGATION_SCOPES.

Fix: In Settings, add is_oauth_scope_allowed(scope_url) that checks against oauth_scopes instead. Use this method in credential_router.resolve() when the provider is OAuthRefreshProvider (or when credential_mode != 'sa+dwd').

Simpler approach: since OAUTH_SCOPES is already validated and used to request consent at login, and the consent check (record.scopes) already enforces per-user limits, the allowlist check for OAuth providers may be redundant. Consider: 
- For SA provider: no scope check needed (SA scopes are internal)
- For DWD provider: check against delegation_scopes (existing behavior)
- For OAuth provider: check against oauth_scopes OR skip (consent check handles it)

The clearest fix is to pass the allowlist into the route entry at construction time (router already has the settings), and check the right allowlist per provider type.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:32Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:32Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.4","title":"Docs/ops: session revoke-all does not revoke OAuth refresh token — user not fully locked out","description":"POST /api/admin/sessions/revoke-all marks Firestore session tokens as revoked but does NOT call credential_router.on_logout(), so the user's Google OAuth refresh token remains valid.

An operator revoking all sessions to lock out a compromised account believes the user is locked out. In OAuth modes (sa+oauth, oauth), the user retains a live refresh token that ExtraSuite's server could still use.","notes":"The /api/auth/oauth/revoke endpoint is the correct way to revoke the OAuth token, but it requires the user to call it themselves (requires a valid session token). Admins have no direct way to revoke another user's OAuth token.

Options:
1. Add an admin endpoint: POST /api/admin/oauth/revoke?email=<email> (admin-only)
2. Have revoke_all_sessions optionally also revoke the OAuth token  
3. At minimum: document this clearly in .env.template and server/CLAUDE.md

Short-term fix (for this PR): Add a prominent note in .env.template and CLAUDE.md explaining the gap. Long-term: consider admin OAuth revocation endpoint.

Also update the revoke_all_sessions response to include a warning when credential_mode != 'sa+dwd':
  {'revoked_count': N, 'oauth_token_revoked': False, 'warning': 'OAuth refresh token not revoked. Use /api/auth/oauth/revoke or delete from Firestore manually.'}","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:45Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:45Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.5","title":"Chore: consolidate duplicate OAuth SA scope maps in config.py and credential_router.py","description":"The OAuth scope mapping for SA commands exists in two places:
- config.py:18-30 as _OAUTH_SA_SCOPE_NAMES (short names)
- credential_router.py:42-54 as _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES (full URLs)

When a new SA command is added, both must be updated or they drift.","notes":"Fix: Remove _OAUTH_SA_SCOPE_NAMES from config.py. The get_oauth_sa_scope_url() method in Settings is only called from tests — if not used in production code, remove it. Have credential_router.py be the single source of truth via _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES.

Check if config.get_oauth_sa_scope_url() is actually called anywhere in production code:
  grep -r 'get_oauth_sa_scope_url' server/src/

If only in tests, remove the method from Settings and update the tests to use credential_router._OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES directly.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:53Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:25:53Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.7","title":"Docs: update server/CLAUDE.md — stale function reference and missing checklist step","description":"server/CLAUDE.md has two issues after this branch:
1. The 'Adding a new command — checklist' section references 'command_registry.resolve_credentials()' which no longer exists — it is now 'credential_router.resolve()'
2. The checklist doesn't mention updating _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES in credential_router.py when adding a new SA command in oauth mode","notes":"Fix 1: Update the resolve_credentials() reference to credential_router.resolve() in the auth flow description.

Fix 2: Add a step to the 'Adding a new command' checklist:
  'If the new command is an SA-class command and CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth is supported, add the command type and its OAuth scope URL to _OAUTH_SA_COMMAND_SCOPES in credential_router.py'

Also update the Firestore Collections table to show the new fields in the 'users' collection:
- encrypted_refresh_token: AES-256-GCM encrypted OAuth refresh token (present when CREDENTIAL_MODE != sa+dwd)
- refresh_token_scopes: Space-separated scope URLs consented at login
- refresh_token_updated_at: When the refresh token was last updated","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:07Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:07Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.8","title":"Docs: add key rotation runbook for OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY","description":"There is no documentation on what happens when OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY must be rotated (compliance, compromise). All stored refresh tokens become undecryptable and every user must re-authenticate simultaneously.","notes":"Add a note to .env.template and/or server/CLAUDE.md (or website docs) covering:

1. What happens during key rotation: all refresh tokens in Firestore are encrypted with the old key. The server will fail to decrypt them with the new key, returning OAuthTokenError → HTTP 500. Users will see 'OAuth credentials expired or revoked — run extrasuite auth login'.

2. Impact: All users must re-authenticate. This is expected behavior for key rotation.

3. Recommended procedure:
   a. Deploy new server with NEW_OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY (double-key period)
   b. Write a migration script that reads each user document, decrypts with old key, re-encrypts with new key, and updates Firestore
   c. Once all tokens are migrated, switch to new key only
   
4. Emergency procedure (immediate rotation):
   a. Update Cloud Run secret to new key
   b. Redeploy server
   c. All users re-authenticate over next 30 days as their session tokens are used
   d. Monitor re-authentication rate in access_logs

Add a short note to .env.template near OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY: 'WARNING: Rotating this key requires re-encrypting all refresh tokens in Firestore or all users must re-authenticate. See deployment docs.'","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:22Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:22Z"}

{"id":"extrasuite-26z.9","title":"Docs: update website docs for OAuth credential modes and new endpoints","description":"website/docs/api/auth-spec.md and website/docs/security.md do not document:
- Three credential modes (sa+dwd, sa+oauth, oauth) and their tradeoffs
- POST /api/auth/oauth/revoke endpoint
- Loss of agent attribution in Drive version history when using CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth
- Required OAUTH_SCOPES and OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY configuration","notes":"Files to update:
1. website/docs/api/auth-spec.md:
   - Add section: Credential Modes
   - Document sa+dwd (default), sa+oauth, oauth with tradeoffs table
   - Add POST /api/auth/oauth/revoke to endpoint list
   - Add the new OAUTH_SCOPES and OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY config vars

2. website/docs/security.md:
   - Add note: CREDENTIAL_MODE=oauth loses per-agent attribution in Drive version history
   - Add note: OAuth refresh tokens stored encrypted in Firestore; encryption key management
   - Add note: session revocation vs OAuth token revocation — they are separate

Tradeoffs table to include:
| Mode | Agent attribution | DWD needed | Refresh token stored |
|---|---|---|---|
| sa+dwd | Yes | Yes | No |
| sa+oauth | Yes | No | Yes (encrypted) |
| oauth | No | No | Yes (encrypted) |","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"Sripathi.Krishnan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:32Z","created_by":"Sripathi Krishnan","updated_at":"2026-03-09T15:26:32Z"}
